src/components/ui-new/widgets/Federation.title: Sub-9px text (7px/8px) across 27 files is below legible/accessible minimums
src/components/ui-new/widgets/Federation.detail: 27 files use `text-[7px]` or `text-[8px]` for body labels. Example FederationPanel.tsx:206-221 renders four stat labels at `text-[7px] text-slate-500` ('Members', 'Learnings', 'Adopted', 'Exchanges'). 7-8px text combined with low-contrast slate-500 on a dark panel is effectively illegible and fails WCAG legibility expectations. Counts: text-[8px] x159, text-[7px] x26. This is the practical fallout of the text-xs override forcing devs ever-smaller.
src/components/ui-new/widgets/Federation.fix: Bump the smallest labels to at least 10px and raise contrast (slate-400 or lighter on dark). Best handled as part of the type-scale remediation above. Per-file change is low-blast but touches visual layout in dense widgets, so verify each panel still fits after enlarging.

src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.title: Mill emoji used outside the three CLAUDE.md-sanctioned branding spots
src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.detail: Line 48 `🏭` renders the mill emoji as a branding mark in the mobile rotate-device overlay footer ('MillOS Digital Twin'). CLAUDE.md permits 🏭 ONLY in: favicon (index.html), loading screen icon (index.html), and the top-left header logo (UIOverlay.tsx). This overlay is none of those. It is branding (spirit-aligned) but violates the literal rule and is inconsistent with the no-emoji-in-UI policy that mandates Lucide icons elsewhere (the same file already imports RotateCcw from lucide-react).
src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.fix: Replace the 🏭 span with a Lucide icon (e.g. `import { Factory } from 'lucide-react';` then `<Factory className="w-4 h-4" />`), matching the no-emoji-use-icons rule. Pure local change, no behavior impact, but it is a visual swap so flag for owner aesthetic approval.

src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.title: No prefers-reduced-motion guard anywhere in scope; indefinite looping animations run unconditionally
src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.detail: A `prefers-reduced-motion` / `useReducedMotion` / `MotionConfig` grep returns ZERO hits across all in-scope files (ui-new, mobile, modals) despite framer-motion driving every modal/panel transition plus CSS infinite loops. Concrete indefinite-loop instances: RotateDeviceOverlay.tsx:25 `<div className="w-24 h-36 border-4 border-cyan-400 rounded-2xl relative animate-pulse">` and :32 `<div className="absolute -right-8 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 text-cyan-400 animate-bounce">`. Users who set 'reduce motion' at the OS level get no relief. Vestibular accessibility (WCAG 2.3.3 AAA / 2.2.2 for the looping pulse/bounce).
src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.fix: Gate looping animations on reduced-motion: e.g. wrap framer-motion app region in `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user">`, and for the CSS loops add a `motion-reduce:animate-none` Tailwind variant to the animate-pulse (line 25) / animate-bounce (line 32) / any animate-spin elements. Changes motion behavior under user preference, so owner should confirm scope.

src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.title: RotateDeviceOverlay (z-[100]) sits below modals (z-[1000]); a modal open during rotation would obscure the rotate prompt
src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.detail: RotateDeviceOverlay uses 'fixed inset-0 z-[100]' (line 17), but KeyboardShortcutsModal (z-[1000], KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx:77) and DecisionReplay (z-[1000], DecisionReplay.tsx:75) sit above it. If a full-screen modal is open and the device rotates to an unsupported orientation, the 'please rotate' overlay renders behind the modal and the user sees a stale modal instead of the rotate prompt. Low co-occurrence (these modals are desktop-oriented), hence low confidence.
src/components/mobile/RotateDeviceOverla.fix: Raise RotateDeviceOverlay to a z-index above all app modals (e.g. z-[2000]) since it is a hard blocking state that should supersede everything.

src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx.title: React LoadingScreen uses 🏭 emoji; CLAUDE.md sanctions the loading-screen emoji only in index.html
src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx.detail:
Line 63 `
🏭
`. CLAUDE.md allows the mill emoji in the 'Loading screen icon (index.html)'. This is a separate React component (src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx), not the index.html inline loading screen. Borderline: it IS a loading screen so it matches the intent of the exception, but the literal rule names index.html. Reporting at low severity / moderate confidence as a consistency note, not a hard violation.
src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx.fix: Acceptable to leave as-is (matches the loading-screen exception intent). If strict compliance is desired, replace with a Lucide Factory icon to match the rest of the UI. Owner decision; no functional impact.

src/components/App.tsx.title: Palette inconsistency: 69 stray gray-* utilities amid 3901 slate-* (slate is the established standard)
src/components/App.tsx.detail: Color-family scan across src (excluding archive): slate-* = 3901 usages, gray-* = 69, zinc/neutral/stone = 0. Slate is unambiguously the project palette. The 69 gray-* classes (in App.tsx, decisionIcons.tsx, SCADAPanel.tsx, WorkerSpeechBubble.tsx, WorkerDetailPanel.tsx, TruckScheduleWidget.tsx, PredictiveMaintenancePanel.tsx, BreakdownEffects.tsx, QCLabRoom.tsx and others) are slightly cooler/different from slate and create subtle visual seams between panels. Tailwind's gray and slate are visually distinct (slate is blue-tinted).
src/components/App.tsx.fix: Migrate the 69 gray-{n} classes to the equivalent slate-{n} (gray-400 -> slate-400, etc.) for palette consistency. Low-risk find-and-replace per-occurrence, but it is a visible color shift, so do it deliberately with visual spot-check rather than a blind global sed. Not auto-fixable without UX review.

src/index.css.title: Focus-visible outline and scrollbar use hardcoded hex instead of theme tokens
src/index.css.detail: src/index.css uses hardcoded colors that bypass the @theme tokens: line 73/83 `outline: 2px solid #22d3ee;` and line 85 `box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.2);` (cyan-400, but --color-mill-cyan is #06b6d4 / cyan-500 — a DIFFERENT cyan), scrollbar thumb line 64 `rgba(100, 116, 139, ...)` (slate-500). The focus ring cyan (#22d3ee) does not match the brand mill-cyan token (#06b6d4), so the focus accent is a slightly different hue than brand accents elsewhere. Minor token/brand drift, not a defect.
src/index.css.fix: Optionally align the focus-visible outline to a defined token (either add a --color-focus-ring token or reuse cyan-400 consistently) so the focus accent matches the brand accent system. Cosmetic; safe to leave for launch.

src/index.css.title: POSITIVE: documented Tailwind v4 universal-reset bug is NOT present (clean)
src/index.css.detail: Per the CLAUDE.md known-bug section, verified there is NO universal `* { margin: 0; padding: 0; }` reset in src/index.css or the index.html inline <style>. index.html line 51 correctly uses scoped `body, html { ... margin: 0; ... }` and `#root { ... }` only. src/index.css base layer (lines 33-46) scopes to html/body/#root. All global base styling is inside `@layer base` / `@layer components` / `@layer utilities`, so cascade-layer precedence is respected and Tailwind utilities are not overridden. The 5 `!important` uses (lines 102-111) are all inside the prefers-reduced-motion media query, which is the standard legitimate a11y use. No action needed — recording as a verified non-issue so it is not re-flagged.
src/index.css.fix: No fix required. This confirms the recurring Tailwind v4 cascade bug is currently absent. Keep universal resets out of index.css and index.html per CLAUDE.md.

src/components/ui/VCLDebugPanel.tsx.title: Emoji legend rendered in VCLDebugPanel UI (debug surface, mirrors vclEncoder protocol)
src/components/ui/VCLDebugPanel.tsx.detail:
Lines 277-304 render emoji directly as UI legend text, e.g. line 286 `
🏛️ Silo | ⚙️ Mill | 🔀 Sifter | 📦 Packer
` and line 303 `
👑 Supervisor | 🔧 Engineer | 👷 Operator | 🛠️ Tech
`. These document the VCP/VCL emoji-encoding protocol in src/utils/vclEncoder.ts, where emoji are an intentional compact data format for LLM context (not decorative UI). This is a developer/debug panel, so it is borderline w.r.t. the no-emoji rule. Reporting low-confidence: the emoji here are a legend FOR an emoji protocol, which is a defensible exception. The vclEncoder.ts emoji themselves are data, not UI, and are NOT a violation.
src/components/ui/VCLDebugPanel.tsx.fix: Acceptable to leave — this is a debug panel documenting an emoji-based protocol. If strict no-emoji compliance is wanted even in debug surfaces, gate the panel behind a dev flag (it may already be) and/or note the exception. No launch-blocking action.

index.html.title: Loading screen relies on 'Inter' webfont before it loads; brief fallback flash on first paint
index.html.detail: index.html inline loading screen sets `font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif` (line 67) and renders immediately in <body> (lines 75-79) BEFORE the Google Fonts stylesheet (line 46, display=swap) has resolved. On first/cold load the loading text paints in the system sans fallback then swaps to Inter (FOUT). This is the documented tradeoff of display=swap (correct choice — avoids invisible text), so it is expected behavior, not a bug. The CSS font stack lacks an explicit metric-compatible fallback (e.g. no `system-ui` before sans-serif on the loading-text rule), making the swap slightly more noticeable than the app's @layer base stack which does include system-ui/-apple-system.
index.html.fix: Optionally add `system-ui, -apple-system` to the loading-text font stack (line 67) to match the base stack and minimize the swap jump. Cosmetic micro-optimization; safe to ship as-is.

index.html.title: CSP connect-src omits PeerJS cloud API host (https://0.peerjs.com) -> multiplayer signaling blocked
index.html.detail: connect-src is: "connect-src 'self' ws: wss: https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com https://cdn.jsdelivr.net https://fonts.gstatic.com https://fonts.googleapis.com;". PeerJS (src/multiplayer/SignalingService.ts uses the default cloud config) defaults to CLOUD_HOST="0.peerjs.com", CLOUD_PORT=443 (verified in node_modules/peerjs/dist/peerjs.min.js). The initial peer-discovery handshake is an HTTPS XHR/fetch to https://0.peerjs.com:443 which is NOT matched by 'self', ws:, or wss:. CSP will block it, so new Peer() never reaches 'open' and WebRTC multiplayer fails on the live GH-Pages site (where this meta CSP is enforced). The bare wss: token does cover the subsequent signaling WebSocket, so only the HTTPS API call is missing.
index.html.fix: Add https://0.peerjs.com to connect-src (and https://*.peerjs.com if self-hosting is ever swapped in). STUN/TURN (stun.l.google.com, *.turn.peerjs.com) are governed by ICE, not connect-src, so no CSP change needed for those.

index.html.title: Only an inline SVG-emoji favicon; no PNG/ICO favicon, theme-color, or apple-touch-icon
index.html.detail: Line 16 is the sole icon: `<link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg ...><text ...>🏭</text></svg>" />`. There is no <meta name="theme-color">, no <link rel="apple-touch-icon">, and no raster favicon/ICO in public/ (find found none). Result: blank/generic icon in Safari/iOS home-screen and many bookmark contexts, no browser-chrome theming color, and no Apple touch icon for the digital-twin app you intend people to keep open.
index.html.fix: Add <meta name="theme-color" content="#0a0f1a">, a public/apple-touch-icon.png (180x180) with <link rel="apple-touch-icon">, and a public/favicon-32x32.png/favicon.ico fallback.

index.html.title: CSP connect-src permits cleartext ws: alongside wss:
index.html.detail: Line 11 connect-src includes both `ws:` and `wss:`. On an HTTPS GH-Pages origin, a cleartext ws: connection would be a mixed-content violation anyway, so `ws:` grants nothing useful and only widens the policy. Tightening to wss: only removes a needless cleartext allowance with no runtime UX change (PeerJS signaling uses wss).
index.html.fix: Remove the bare `ws:` token from connect-src, leaving `wss:` (plus the explicit https hosts).

index.html.title: CSP lacks upgrade-insecure-requests / object-src / base-uri / form-action hardening
index.html.detail: The meta CSP (lines 5-13) has default-src/script-src/style-src/etc. but omits `object-src 'none'`, `base-uri 'self'`, `form-action 'self'`, and `upgrade-insecure-requests`. GH-Pages cannot send the security response headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options) so the meta CSP is the only control surface; without object-src/base-uri the policy leaves plugin and <base>-injection vectors open, and without upgrade-insecure-requests any accidental http:// subresource is not auto-upgraded.
index.html.fix: Add `object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'; upgrade-insecure-requests;` to the CSP. Also consider `frame-ancestors 'none'` since X-Frame-Options can't be set on GH-Pages.

index.html.title: No JSON-LD structured data despite a well-defined product (missed SEO opportunity)
index.html.detail: index.html has good OG/Twitter/canonical (lines 22-41) and a meta description, but no <script type="application/ld+json"> structured data. The app is a clearly-scoped SoftwareApplication/WebApplication ('AI-Powered Grain Mill Digital Twin Simulator', browser-based, by Nell Watson) that would qualify for a WebApplication/SoftwareApplication schema, improving rich-result eligibility for a public launch.
index.html.fix: Add a JSON-LD block describing a WebApplication (name MillOS, applicationCategory, operatingSystem 'Any (Web)', author, url https://millos.net/, image og-image.png). Pure additive metadata, no runtime effect.

index.html.title: Google Fonts leaks visitor IP to Google
index.html.detail: Lines 44-46 load fonts.googleapis.com/gstatic.com; visitor IP sent to Google pre-consent (GDPR LG Muenchen 2022). MedievalSharp.ttf self-hosted line 49.
index.html.fix: Self-host woff2, remove googleapis links and CSP font hosts.

index.html.title: CSP whitelists cdn.jsdelivr.net but src never fetches it
index.html.detail: connect-src has cdn.jsdelivr.net; no runtime fetch in src; deps bundled. Over-permissive.
index.html.fix: Remove cdn.jsdelivr.net from connect-src in index.html:11.

index.html.title: connect-src allows wildcard ws:/wss: to any host
index.html.detail: connect-src is 'self' ws: wss: https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com https://cdn.jsdelivr.net https://fonts.gstatic.com https://fonts.googleapis.com. Bare 'ws:' and 'wss:' permit WebSocket connections to ANY origin. With PeerJS (src/multiplayer/SignalingService.ts:26-30) using the default public cloud server and untrusted WebRTC peers, this lets injected/compromised script (or a malicious peer-delivered payload) exfiltrate to arbitrary WS endpoints. The only WS endpoint actually needed is the PeerJS cloud signaler (0.peerjs.com over wss) plus any self-hosted SCADA WS.
index.html.fix: Replace bare 'ws: wss:' with the specific signaling host, e.g. 'wss://0.peerjs.com' (and the user's SCADA host if applicable). If a self-hosted PeerServer is later used, add that exact host. Keep 'ws:' only if local-dev SCADA over plain ws is required, scoped to localhost.

index.html.title: cdn.jsdelivr.net in connect-src enables remote font-data fetch from third party (supply-chain surface)
index.html.detail: connect-src includes 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net'. troika-three-text (bundled via @react-three/drei/three-fiber) fetches Unicode font-resolver data at runtime from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/lojjic/unicode-font-resolver@v1.0.1/packages/data' (seen verbatim in built bundle public/v0.20/assets/three-fiber-*.js and the dev module). This is connect-src data (not script-src remote code), so it cannot execute arbitrary JS, but it makes glyph rendering depend on a third-party CDN and adds an exfiltration-eligible host to the policy. If jsdelivr is compromised it can serve crafted JSON, which is parsed but not executed.
index.html.fix: Acceptable to keep if 3D text glyph coverage for non-Latin scripts is needed. To eliminate the dependency, self-host the unicode-font-resolver data and pass a local dataUrl to troika's font resolver (or restrict to the Latin subset shipped locally), then remove cdn.jsdelivr.net from connect-src. Product decision on whether non-Latin glyph coverage is required.

index.html.title: CSP missing object-src, base-uri, and frame-ancestors hardening directives
index.html.detail: The CSP meta tag declares default-src/script-src/style-src/font-src/img-src/connect-src/worker-src but omits 'object-src', 'base-uri', and 'frame-ancestors'. Without 'object-src none', plugin/embed vectors are not explicitly denied (default-src 'self' covers it but explicit is best-practice for XSS hardening). Without 'base-uri self', an injected <base> tag can hijack all relative URLs (the app relies heavily on relative paths — import.meta.env.BASE_URL, sw.js relative precache). frame-ancestors cannot be set via meta tag (it is ignored in meta and only works as an HTTP header), so clickjacking protection is absent on GitHub Pages which sends no X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors header.
index.html.fix: Add 'object-src 'none';' and 'base-uri 'self';' to the CSP meta in index.html. For frame-ancestors/clickjacking, since GitHub Pages cannot set custom response headers, either accept the residual clickjacking risk (low for a single-page simulator with no auth/sensitive actions) or front the site with a CDN/proxy (e.g. Cloudflare) that injects 'Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'' and 'X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN'. Document the limitation.

index.html.title: script-src 'unsafe-inline' is broader than needed and is silently negated by the absence of nonces/hashes
index.html.detail: script-src is ''self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' blob:'. 'unsafe-eval' is genuinely required (Rapier/Basis-transcoder WASM compilation via blob workers, troika). However 'unsafe-inline' permits arbitrary inline <script>, which is the classic XSS amplifier: any injected markup that reaches the DOM can execute. The only inline script-like content in index.html is the module loader <script type=module src> (external, not inline) and an inline <style> (covered by style-src, not script-src), so 'unsafe-inline' for scripts appears unnecessary for first-party code. Note: adding a nonce/hash would itself cause browsers to ignore 'unsafe-inline', so the current value provides no protection while widening the attack surface.
index.html.fix: Attempt removing 'unsafe-inline' from script-src and verify the build still runs (R3F/Vite emit external modules, not inline scripts). If a bundler-injected inline bootstrap exists, switch to a hashed/nonce'd inline script instead. Keep 'unsafe-eval' and 'blob:' (WASM requirement). Verify in a CSP-enforced browser before shipping → flag for owner since it touches runtime behavior.

index.html.title: CSP delivered only via meta tag (no enforcement of frame-ancestors/Report-Only/upgrade-insecure)
index.html.detail: The entire security policy ships as <meta http-equiv> tags (CSP line 5, X-Content-Type-Options line 14, referrer line 15). Meta-delivered CSP is parsed only after the parser reaches it and silently ignores frame-ancestors, sandbox, and report-uri/report-to directives. On GitHub Pages there is no way to add HTTP response headers, so X-Frame-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, and frame-ancestors are entirely absent. Combined with 'connect-src ws: wss:' wildcard, the policy is weaker than it appears.
index.html.fix: Document that GitHub Pages cannot serve security headers and decide whether to front millos.net with a header-injecting CDN (Cloudflare/Netlify) to add HSTS + frame-ancestors + a header-delivered CSP. If staying on GH Pages, keep the meta CSP but acknowledge frame-ancestors/HSTS gaps in the launch risk register. Product/infra decision.

src/components/AlertSystem.tsx.title: Sample alert machineId 'mill-1.5' and 'sifter-0' are invalid machine IDs
src/components/AlertSystem.tsx.detail: getSampleAlerts() uses `machineId: 'mill-1.5'` (line 13) for a 'Roller Mill #3 back online' message and `machineId: 'sifter-0'` (line 19) for a 'Plansifter #2' message. Neither id exists (valid: rm-101..106, sifter-a/b/c). Any UI logic keying off these machineIds will not match a real machine. Note 'Roller Mill #3' should map to rm-103, 'Plansifter #2' to sifter-b.
src/components/AlertSystem.tsx.fix: Set `machineId: 'rm-103'` (line 13) and `machineId: 'sifter-b'` (line 19) to match the message text. Stays inside AlertSystem.tsx.

src/components/physics/PhysicsForklift.t.title: Physics forklift registers isStopped=false while halted by standalone emergency stop
src/components/physics/PhysicsForklift.t.detail: Physics is default-ON (graphicsStore.ts:122 `enablePhysics: true`), so PhysicsForklift is the live path. Line 131: `const isStopped = emergencyDrillMode || operationRef.current !== 'traveling';` — it omits forkliftEmergencyStop. When the standalone E-stop is engaged (EmergencyStopButton.tsx toggles setForkliftEmergencyStop), the forklift is physically halted at line 152-154 (`if (emergencyDrillMode || forkliftEmergencyStop) { rb.setLinvel({x:0,y:0,z:0}); ... }`), but it then registers isStopped=false into positionRegistry. Workers querying the registry (isForkliftApproaching / nearest-forklift stopped state) treat the halted forklift as moving and may needlessly evade a stationary forklift. The code comment at line 129-130 even states isStopped 'should include emergency drill mode AND loading/unloading' but forgets the standalone E-stop.
src/components/physics/PhysicsForklift.t.fix: Read forkliftEmergencyStop in updatePosition and include it: `const isStopped = emergencyDrillMode || forkliftEmergencyStop || operationRef.current !== 'traveling';` (forkliftEmergencyStop is already subscribed at line 97). Add it to the updatePosition useCallback deps as well.

src/components/ForkliftSystem.tsx.title: Legacy forklift wheel refs go stale after a close→far→close LOD cycle (wheels stop spinning)
src/components/ForkliftSystem.tsx.detail: Legacy (non-physics) path only. Lines 874-876: `if (wheelRefsRef.current.length === 0 && ref.current.children.length >= 12) { wheelRefsRef.current = ref.current.children.slice(8, 12) as THREE.Mesh[]; }`. The capture guard only fires while the array is empty. On a close→far→close LOD round-trip, ForkliftModel unmounts (replaced by ForkliftBillboard at distanceTier 'far') and remounts with NEW child meshes, but wheelRefsRef still holds the old (now-detached) meshes and the `length === 0` guard never re-fires, so the loop at 877-879 mutates detached meshes and the visible wheels stop animating. Visual-only and legacy-only.
src/components/ForkliftSystem.tsx.fix: Reset wheelRefsRef.current to [] when distanceTier transitions back to 'close' (e.g. in a useEffect on distanceTier), or re-capture when the cached mesh is no longer parented to ref.current. Visual change — verify in-app.

src/components/ForkliftSystem.tsx.title: Unguarded pathActions index access can throw if path/pathActions lengths diverge
src/components/ForkliftSystem.tsx.detail: Line 839: `const action = data.pathActions[pathIndexRef.current];` followed by `action.type` reads at 842/849. pathIndexRef is bounded by `% data.path.length` (line 858), NOT by pathActions.length. The two hard-coded forklifts currently have matching lengths (8/8 and 6/6) so there is no live crash, but if path and pathActions ever diverge (e.g. a new route edited in one array only), action is undefined and `action.type` throws inside useFrame, killing the render loop. Defensive gap, not a live failure.
src/components/ForkliftSystem.tsx.fix: Guard: `const action = data.pathActions[pathIndexRef.current]; if (!action) { pathIndexRef.current = (pathIndexRef.current + 1) % data.path.length; currentTarget.current.set(...data.path[pathIndexRef.current]); return; }` — or assert path.length === pathActions.length at construction.

src/components/machines/UIComponents.tsx.title: Machine panel re-registers under a fresh random id on every status change
src/components/machines/UIComponents.tsx.detail: Lines 41-55: the registration effect depends on [enabled, status] and computes `const id = `panel-${Math.random()}`` inside the effect body. On each status transition (running→warning→critical, which is routine), the effect tears down (unregisterPanel(prevId)) and re-registers under a brand-new random id, churning panelRegistry entries. Registration is also conditional (`if (screenMaterial.current)`) while cleanup is unconditional, so an unregister can run for an id that was never registered (harmless no-op). No leak because each effect run closes over its own id, but the random-id-per-status pattern is fragile and makes the registry key non-deterministic.
src/components/machines/UIComponents.tsx.fix: Generate a stable id once with useId() or a useRef, e.g. `const idRef = useRef(`panel-${useId()}`)`, and use idRef.current in both register and unregister so the same panel keeps one identity across status changes.

src/utils/audioManager.ts.title: Muting does not stop an in-flight TTS PA announcement
src/utils/audioManager.ts.detail: The `set muted(value)` setter (lines 289-296) updates master/music/machine WebAudio gains but never touches the SpeechSynthesis TTS pipeline: `set muted(value: boolean) { this._muted = value; this.updateMasterVolume(); this.updateMusicVolume(); this.updateMachineVolumes(); this.saveSettings(); this.notifyListeners(); }`. TTS announcements are spoken via `window.speechSynthesis.speak(utterance)` (line 5620), which is NOT routed through the WebAudio gain graph, so zeroing gains does not silence it. By contrast `set ttsEnabled(false)` calls `this.stopTTS()` (line 5329). The queue-processing guard at line 5486 (`if (!this._ttsEnabled || this._muted)`) and the scheduler guards already block NEW announcements when muted, so the bug is bounded to exactly ONE already-speaking utterance continuing to talk after the user mutes. Clearly-wrong UX: muting should silence the voice immediately.
src/utils/audioManager.ts.fix: Call `this.stopTTS()` inside the muted setter when value===true (stopTTS already cancels speechSynthesis, clears the queue, and clears the chime timeout). This changes mute semantics, so it needs owner sign-off.

src/utils/audioManager.ts.title: Production TTS pipeline emits ~14 unconditional console.log debug lines
src/utils/audioManager.ts.detail: The announcement/TTS path logs verbosely to the raw console regardless of environment: e.g. line 5480 `console.log('[TTS] Queue empty, stopping')`, 5487 `console.log('[TTS] Blocked in queue processing:', {...})`, 5510, 5559 `console.log('[TTS] Speech STARTED')`, 5565, 5593, 5602, 5614-5619, 5624. These use `console.log` directly rather than the project's gated `audioLog`/`logger` abstraction used elsewhere in the same file (e.g. audioLog.warn at 5580/5631). On every PA announcement this spams the production console at millos.net. Not a crash, but console noise and minor info leak of internal state.
src/utils/audioManager.ts.fix: Route these through the existing gated logger (audioLog.debug) or guard with import.meta.env.DEV. Mechanical, no runtime UX change.

src/utils/audioManager.ts.title: 29 ungated console.log in TTS in prod
src/utils/audioManager.ts.detail: 29 raw console.log ungated, echo text on every announcement. logger.audio exists.

src/utils/aiEngine.ts.title: AI engine teardown leaks pending safety-report resolution timeouts
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.detail: `initializeAIEngine()`'s returned cleanup (lines 3390-3398) clears `loopInterval` but never clears the module-level `activeResolutionTimeouts` map (declared line 3978, populated line 4109 with 5000ms setTimeouts via `activeResolutionTimeouts.set(report.id, timeoutId)`). After teardown these timeouts still fire and call `useSafetyReportStore.getState().resolveSafetyReport(...)` against a store that may belong to a torn-down session. The debounceTimeout in initializeDecisionOutcomeTracking IS cleaned up (line 3028) — this map is the inconsistent gap. Low impact (one-shot 5s timers, store calls are idempotent) but a genuine lifecycle leak.
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.fix: In the cleanup closure, add: `for (const id of activeResolutionTimeouts.keys()) clearTimeout(activeResolutionTimeouts.get(id)!); activeResolutionTimeouts.clear();` before/after clearing loopInterval. Stays inside the file; no behavior change in normal operation.

src/utils/aiEngine.ts.title: Strategic Gemini failures never trigger API exponential backoff
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.detail: `generateStrategicDecision()` swallows its own errors: `catch (error) { logger.error('[Strategic] Decision generation failed:', error); setStrategicThinking(false); return null; }` (lines 3951-3955) and returns null instead of rethrowing. The AI-loop caller wraps it in try/catch (lines 3292-3308) expecting to call `recordApiFailure()` on throw — but because the error is swallowed and null returned, the loop sees a benign null and `recordApiFailure()` (line 3304) never runs for strategic API errors. So repeated Gemini failures on the strategic path do not increment apiBackoff.consecutiveFailures, defeating the backoff for that layer. NOTE: do NOT route the loop-level null to recordApiFailure — null is also the benign no-op return (not active / not connected / no priorities).
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.fix: Add `recordApiFailure();` inside the catch block at line 3952 (the genuine-error site), alongside the existing logger.error. Local, single-file.

src/utils/aiEngine.ts.title: No AbortController on in-flight Gemini strategic call — late response can resolve after config/key change
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.detail: generateStrategicDecision (aiEngine.ts:3891) awaits geminiClient.generateContent(prompt) with no AbortController/cancellation. If the user clears their key or disconnects (clearGeminiConfig at aiConfigStore.ts:527 calls geminiClient.disconnect()) while a request is in flight, the awaited promise still resolves, recordApiUsage runs (:3900) charging the cost overlay, and setStrategicPriorities/recordDecision fire for a now-disconnected client. The legacy SDK's generateContent does accept an AbortSignal via requestOptions, so this is wireable. Low because it is a transient correctness/cost-accounting issue, not a crash.
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.fix: Thread an AbortController through geminiClient.generateContent (pass { signal } as the SDK requestOptions second arg) and abort it from disconnect(). At minimum, re-check geminiClient.isConnected() after the await before recording cost/priorities.

src/utils/aiEngine.ts.title: Emoji in user-facing AI decision text violates project No-Emoji rule
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.detail: CLAUDE.md mandates 'Never use emoji characters in the codebase' (only the 🏭 branding exception). aiEngine.ts:3921 sets `action: \`🧠 Strategic: ${strategic.priorities[0]}\``, and :3913-3914 build reasoning text with 💡 and ⚖️. These strings flow into AIDecision.action/reasoning and are rendered verbatim in the AI decision panels (AICommandCenter). Line 3756 even has to strip the '🧠 Strategic: ' prefix back out, confirming it is real rendered content. Lane-marginal and cosmetic, but a genuine guideline violation in shipped UI text.
src/utils/aiEngine.ts.fix: Replace the emoji prefixes with Lucide icons in the rendering component (per CLAUDE.md), or drop the inline emoji and keep the label plain ('Strategic: ...', 'Insight: ...', 'Trade-off: ...'). Changes displayed text — flag for owner.

src/components/AmbientDetails.tsx.title: Roof-leak drip uses setState every animation frame (stale read, re-render churn)
src/components/AmbientDetails.tsx.detail: RoofLeakPuddle's animation callback (lines 4172-4193) calls `setDropY((prev) => ...)` every frame, contradicting this file's own stated design (line 915 comment: condensation is 'ref-based animation (no setState in useFrame)'). Per-frame setState forces a React re-render of the component each frame. Additionally `dropRef.current.position.y = dropY` (line 4183) reads the stale closure `dropY`, not the freshly computed `newY` returned from the updater, so the mesh visually lags the state by one animation tick. Throttled to every 3rd frame by the manager, so impact is modest, but it is the only per-frame setState in the centralized-animation file.
src/components/AmbientDetails.tsx.fix: Convert dropY to a useRef (e.g. dropYRef.current) updated/read inside the callback, matching the condensation-drip pattern, eliminating per-frame setState and the stale read. Visual/3D change — verify the drip still animates; prefer owner review.

src/components/AmbientDetails.tsx.title: Door-toggle interval restarts on every door state change (timing churn)
src/components/AmbientDetails.tsx.detail: AmbientDetailsGroup's door interval useEffect (lines 4532-4556) lists `[doorStates]` as its dependency. Each door toggle calls `setDoorStates(...)`, which changes doorStates, which tears down and recreates the interval — re-rolling the random `15000 + Math.random()*30000` delay on every fire. Net effect: the 'periodic' door behavior never settles to its intended cadence; the timer is effectively reset each cycle. (Sound logic itself is correct: pre-flip `doorStates[doorId]===true` -> playDoorClose, ===false -> playDoorOpen, which matches the open/close transition.) Not a correctness defect in output, only timing/perf.
src/components/AmbientDetails.tsx.fix: Use a functional setState inside the callback to read current door state and drop `doorStates` from deps (use [] so the interval is created once), or read state from a ref. Local single-file change; low risk but alters door cadence slightly, so light owner awareness.

src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.title: Entire apiSecurity module (CSRF/JWT/secureFetch) is dead — intentional 'future auth' scaffolding in a no-backend app
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.detail: Every exported symbol has ZERO importers anywhere in src outside its own file and the (also-dead) utils barrel. Grep counts (src + test): secureFetch=0/0, generateCsrfToken=0/0, getOrCreateCsrfToken=0/0, clearCsrfToken=0/0, decodeTokenPayload=0/0, isTokenExpired=0/0, getTokenExpiresIn=0/0, validateRequest=0/0, maskSensitiveData=0/0, clearSensitiveFields=0/0, rateLimiters=0/0, checkRateLimit=0/0, debounce=0/0, throttle=0/0. The file self-documents the intent at line 7: 'Token management utilities (for future auth integration)' and line 249 'JWT-like token structure (for future auth integration)', with OWASP refs. This is intentionally-dead scaffolding, not accidental dead code. Real cost: 642 lines of unused CSRF/JWT/secureFetch in an app the task states has NO backend auth can mislead a future security reviewer into believing auth/CSRF protection exists.
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.fix: Owner decision: either (a) keep as a deliberately-documented library and add a top-of-file banner '// UNUSED: scaffolding for future server-side auth — not wired into any runtime path', or (b) delete src/utils/apiSecurity.ts and its barrel re-export block (utils/index.ts:123-147) to shed 642 dead lines. Do NOT delete without the product/security call since it advertises a security posture.

src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.title: secureFetch() exported but never used; RESTAdapter uses raw fetch instead
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.detail: secureFetch() (rate limiting, CSRF header injection, timeout, same-origin credentials) is defined here and re-exported via utils/index.ts:136, but has ZERO consumers project-wide (grep across src/ minus the def+barrel returns nothing). The actual SCADA caller src/scada/adapters/RESTAdapter.ts:332 uses raw `fetch(url, { ...options, signal })` and manually sets `Authorization: Bearer ${this.config.apiKey}` at line 354 rather than secureFetch. So the 'secure' wrapper is dead scaffolding.
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.fix: Either delete secureFetch + its SecureFetchOptions type and remove from the barrel (dead-code cleanup), or have RESTAdapter adopt it. For launch, removing the unused export is the low-risk path; adopting it in RESTAdapter changes runtime request behavior and needs an owner call.

src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.title: CSRF helpers (generate/getOrCreate/clearCsrfToken) exported but never used — dead scaffolding
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.detail: generateCsrfToken (344), getOrCreateCsrfToken (361), clearCsrfToken (375) are only consumed internally by secureFetch (line 553), which itself has no callers. Grep for these three across src/ (minus def+barrel) returns nothing. With no backend/server auth (frontend-only GitHub Pages app), client-side CSRF tokens validate against nothing. They are dead exports re-exported via index.ts:132-134.
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.fix: Remove the three CSRF exports from utils/index.ts and delete the unused functions (and CSRF_TOKEN_KEY) once secureFetch is also removed. Pure dead-code deletion; no runtime behavior depends on them.

src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.title: JWT helpers (decodeTokenPayload/isTokenExpired/getTokenExpiresIn) exported but never used
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.detail: decodeTokenPayload (268), isTokenExpired (306), getTokenExpiresIn (323) and TokenPayload type are 'for future auth integration' (per file header) but have ZERO consumers — grep across src/ minus def+barrel returns nothing. The app has no auth/JWT flow (frontend-only, no backend). Re-exported via index.ts:129-131. Dead scaffolding shipping to production.
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.fix: Remove these three exports + TokenPayload from utils/index.ts and delete the unused functions, or move to a clearly-marked future/ stub. Dead-code cleanup, no runtime impact.

src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.title: rateLimiters (gemini/chat/connect) exported but never wired — no rate limiting active
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.detail: rateLimiters.gemini/.chat/.connect are pre-built wrappers intended to throttle Gemini calls, chat spam, and reconnection storms, but grep for `rateLimiters.` across src/ (minus def+barrel) returns nothing. So untrusted-peer chat (sendChat at MultiplayerManager.ts:596) has no spam limit and Gemini calls have no client-side quota guard despite the wrappers existing. checkRateLimit itself is also unused outside this file.
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.fix: Wire rateLimiters.chat around sendChat() in MultiplayerManager.ts and rateLimiters.gemini around geminiClient.generateContent calls, OR remove the unused exports. Wiring changes runtime behavior (messages/requests can now be dropped) so it needs an owner decision; the removal path is the safe launch option.

src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.title: maskSensitiveData / clearSensitiveFields exported but never used; key masking done ad hoc
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.detail: maskSensitiveData (612) and clearSensitiveFields (633) are exported via index.ts:137-138 but have ZERO consumers. GeminiSettingsModal.tsx:237 displays the key via geminiClient.getMaskedApiKey() (geminiClient.ts:165), which re-implements masking inline (`slice(0,4)...slice(-4)`) instead of using the shared maskSensitiveData. Duplicated logic + dead export.
src/utils/apiSecurity.ts.fix: Either have getMaskedApiKey() delegate to maskSensitiveData (de-dupe), or remove maskSensitiveData/clearSensitiveFields from the barrel as dead code. Consolidation is low-risk but touches geminiClient output format slightly; removal is cleanest for launch.

src/utils/index.ts.title: utils/index.ts barrel has zero importers — every consumer imports source modules directly
src/utils/index.ts.detail: No file in src imports the utils barrel by bare path (no 'from ".../utils"' / 'from "@/utils"'). Spot-checks confirm all consumers go direct: App.tsx:30 `import { audioManager } from './utils/audioManager'`, Machines.tsx:9 `import { getStatusColor } from '../utils/statusColors'`, Machines.tsx:12 `import { ... } from '../utils/sharedMaterials'`. The 147-line barrel re-exports ~80 symbols that nothing consumes through it. This is what makes the dead apiSecurity module appear 'reachable' to a casual reader, though apiSecurity is dead independently.
src/utils/index.ts.fix: Delete src/utils/index.ts (zero runtime impact — never in the bundle since nothing imports it). Tests confirmed not to import it.

src/components/truckbay/test-import.ts.title: truckbay/test-import.ts is a 2-line scratch re-export with zero consumers
src/components/truckbay/test-import.ts.detail: Full contents: `import * as BufferGeometryUtils from 'three/examples/jsm/utils/BufferGeometryUtils.js'; export { BufferGeometryUtils };`. Grep for 'test-import' / imports of '.../truckbay/test-import' returns nothing — no file consumes it. The filename ('test-import') and content signal a one-off import-resolution check left behind. Git-tracked.
src/components/truckbay/test-import.ts.fix: Delete src/components/truckbay/test-import.ts. If BufferGeometryUtils is needed, callers should import it directly from three/examples.

src/components/ui/index.ts.title: ui/index.ts barrel has zero importers — ui-new and UIOverlay reach ui/ panels by direct path, not the barrel
src/components/ui/index.ts.detail: No file imports the components/ui barrel (`from '.../components/ui'`). Consumers use direct paths, e.g. MissionControl.tsx:5 `import { MillClockDisplay } from '../../ui/MillClockDisplay'` and UIOverlay.tsx:66 `import { KeyboardShortcutsModal } from './ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal'`. The 35-line barrel (re-exporting all 28 ui/ panels) is dead and is the sole 'reference' to 4 otherwise-orphaned panels (next finding).
src/components/ui/index.ts.fix: Delete src/components/ui/index.ts (no runtime impact; never bundled). After deletion the 4 barrel-only panels below become provably unreferenced.

src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.title: ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlow.tsx is orphaned — referenced only by the dead ui/index.ts barrel
src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.detail: Repo-wide grep for 'AlertAcknowledgmentFlow' (excluding its own file and Archive) returns exactly one hit: src/components/ui/index.ts. No component, store, test, or dynamic import references it. Since the ui/index.ts barrel itself has zero importers, this panel is fully dead.
src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.fix: Remove the AlertAcknowledgmentFlow export from ui/index.ts and delete src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlow.tsx. Confirm via grep no string-keyed/lazy loader references it (none found).

src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.title: Toast alert container has no aria-live region (status messages not announced)
src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.detail: The alert/toast container is a plain div: `<div className="fixed bottom-4 right-4 z-40 space-y-2 max-w-sm">` (line 64). It renders dynamic warning/critical alerts (alert.title/alert.message, lines 91-92) that appear and disappear, but has no role="region"/aria-live="polite" (and critical alerts have no role="alert"). Screen-reader users receive no notification when an alert is added. Violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.fix: Add role="region" aria-live="polite" aria-label="Notifications" to the container div (and consider role="alert" / aria-live="assertive" for type==='critical' cards). No behavior change.

src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.title: Icon-only alert-dismiss button has no aria-label (title alone insufficient)
src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.detail: The dismiss button contains only an X icon: `<button onClick={() => handleAcknowledge(alert.id)} className="..." title="Acknowledge"><X className="w-3.5 h-3.5 ..." /></button>` (lines 94-100). `title` is not a reliable accessible name for icon-only controls across assistive tech; the button has no aria-label and no text node. Violates WCAG 4.1.2 / 1.1.1.
src/components/ui/AlertAcknowledgmentFlo.fix: Add aria-label="Acknowledge alert" (or include the alert title, e.g. `aria-label={`Acknowledge: ${alert.title}`}`). Keep the existing X icon.

src/components/ui/EmergencyControlPanel..title: ui/EmergencyControlPanel.tsx is orphaned — referenced only by the dead ui/index.ts barrel
src/components/ui/EmergencyControlPanel..detail: Grep for 'EmergencyControlPanel' (excluding own file and Archive) yields only src/components/ui/index.ts. Distinct from EmergencyStopButton (which UIOverlay imports). No live consumer; the lone barrel reference is itself unimported.
src/components/ui/EmergencyControlPanel..fix: Remove the EmergencyControlPanel export from ui/index.ts and delete src/components/ui/EmergencyControlPanel.tsx after confirming no dynamic/string reference.

src/components/ui/EmergencyControlPanel..title: Collapsible section toggle missing aria-expanded (systemic across 10 panels)
src/components/ui/EmergencyControlPanel..detail: The expand/collapse toggle `<button onClick={() => setExpanded(!expanded)} ...>` (line 39-52) conveys state only via a swapped Chevron icon (line 51) with no text/label; it has no aria-expanded. grep confirms aria-expanded appears in 0 of 29 ui/*.tsx while the same toggle pattern exists in EmergencyControlPanel, IncidentHistoryPanel, SafetyAnalyticsPanel, CollapsibleLegend, DecisionHistoryPanel, VCLDebugPanel, ZoneCustomizationPanel, GraphicsSettingsPanel, SafetyConfigPanel, WeatherControlPanel. Screen-reader users aren't told the section is collapsed/expanded. Violates WCAG 4.1.2 (name/role/value).
src/components/ui/EmergencyControlPanel..fix: Add aria-expanded={expanded} to each section-toggle button (and aria-controls pointing at the collapsible content id). Pure state exposure, no behavior change. Apply across all 10 listed files.

src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..title: ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel.tsx is orphaned — referenced only by the dead ui/index.ts barrel (note: ui-new has SettingsPanel)
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..detail: Grep for 'GraphicsSettingsPanel' (excluding own file and Archive) returns only src/components/ui/index.ts. Graphics settings in the live app are handled by the ui-new SettingsPanel; this old-gen panel is unreferenced. Flagged for owner because graphics/quality UI is product-facing and the owner should confirm ui-new fully covers it before deletion.
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..fix: Remove the GraphicsSettingsPanel export from ui/index.ts and delete the file, after the owner confirms ui-new/panels/SettingsPanel supersedes it.

src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..title: Destructive 'Reset Simulation' wipes localStorage and reloads with NO confirmation
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..detail: Lines 621-635: `onClick={() => { localStorage.removeItem('millos-settings'); localStorage.removeItem('millos-game-simulation'); setGraphicsQuality('medium'); window.location.reload(); }}`. A single click on a red button immediately erases persisted progress and force-reloads the page — no `window.confirm()` or in-UI confirmation step. A misclick destroys the user's session permanently.
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..fix: Wrap the handler in a confirmation: `if (!window.confirm('Reset the entire simulation? This clears saved progress and reloads.')) return;` before the removeItem calls, or gate behind a two-step confirm UI like the existing showGeminiConfirmation pattern in GeminiSettingsModal.

src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..title: GraphicsSettingsPanel reset clears wrong/nonexistent keys, so graphics settings survive the reload
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..detail: Line 623 removes 'millos-settings' (nonexistent) and 'millos-game-simulation', then calls setGraphicsQuality('medium') and reloads. Because the real graphics persist key 'millos-graphics' is never cleared, the persist middleware re-hydrates the OLD graphics settings on reload, immediately overwriting the in-memory setGraphicsQuality('medium') call. The graphics 'reset' is therefore ineffective — only the game-sim store is actually reset.
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..fix: Change 'millos-settings' to 'millos-graphics' so the persisted graphics state is cleared before reload; setGraphicsQuality('medium') becomes the rehydrated default.

src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..title: Keyboard shortcuts cheat-sheet shows stale 'A | AI Panel' — actual key is 'I'
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..detail: Lines 514-525 render a kbd 'A' labeled 'AI Panel'. The handler was deliberately moved off A to I to avoid the WASD movement conflict (useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:193 comment 'changed from A to avoid conflict with WASD movement'; line 194 `if (key === 'i')`). Pressing A does nothing for the AI panel; the in-app help is wrong. (App.tsx:581 screen-reader notice correctly says 'I for AI panel', so the two help surfaces disagree.)
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..fix: Change the kbd label from 'A' to 'I' in the Controls grid (and similarly verify 'AI Panel' label text). SCADA ('O') is missing from this list entirely and could be added.

src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..title: 'Reset to 10am' and 'Reset Simulation' sit adjacent with near-identical styling — easy mis-click between mild and destructive action
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..detail: Lines 610-636: a benign amber 'Reset to 10am' button sits directly left of the destructive red 'Reset Simulation' (full localStorage wipe + reload), both same size in a `flex gap-2` row. The only differentiator is amber-vs-red tint at [10px] font. Combined with no confirmation (see related finding), the layout invites destructive mis-clicks. Same adjacency exists in SettingsPanel.tsx:452-473 (stacked).
src/components/ui/GraphicsSettingsPanel..fix: Visually separate the destructive action (own row, more padding, an explicit 'Danger zone' subheading) and/or require confirmation. Pair this with the confirmation-dialog fix.

src/components/ui/WeatherControlPanel.ts.title: ui/WeatherControlPanel.tsx is orphaned — referenced only by the dead ui/index.ts barrel
src/components/ui/WeatherControlPanel.ts.detail: Grep for 'WeatherControlPanel' (excluding own file and Archive) returns only src/components/ui/index.ts. No live consumer (WeatherEffectsOverlay is separate and also barrel-only). The single barrel reference is itself unimported, so this is dead.
src/components/ui/WeatherControlPanel.ts.fix: Remove the WeatherControlPanel export from ui/index.ts and delete the file after confirming no string-keyed reference.

src/components/ui-new/widgets/Management.title: ui-new/widgets/ManagementStylePanel.tsx is a half-finished-migration orphan — zero references anywhere
src/components/ui-new/widgets/Management.detail: Repo-wide grep for 'ManagementStylePanel' and 'ManagementStyle' (excluding its own file and Archive) returns ZERO hits. It is not imported by ContextSidebar.tsx, not registered in sidebar/panelPreloader.ts (which uses string-keyed lazy `import()`s), and not in any test. Built as part of the ui-new migration but never wired into GameInterface.
src/components/ui-new/widgets/Management.fix: Delete src/components/ui-new/widgets/ManagementStylePanel.tsx, or wire it into ContextSidebar/panelPreloader if it was meant to ship. Owner decides intended-but-unwired vs abandoned.

src/components/ui-new/widgets/Preference.title: ui-new/widgets/PreferenceRequestWidget.tsx component is orphaned — its backing data/store IS used, the widget is not
src/components/ui-new/widgets/Preference.detail: Grep for 'PreferenceRequest' shows the type/store machinery is alive (types.ts:332/376, workerMoodStore.ts createPreferenceRequest/grant/deny/triggerRandomPreferenceRequest, aiEngine.ts:4018/4035/4052, WorkerMoodOverlay.tsx:473-515), but the WIDGET COMPONENT (PreferenceRequestWidget.tsx) has zero importers: no hit references the component file outside itself. The preference-request feature renders via WorkerMoodOverlay, not this widget. Not in panelPreloader's string-keyed lazy imports.
src/components/ui-new/widgets/Preference.fix: Delete src/components/ui-new/widgets/PreferenceRequestWidget.tsx if the feature is surfaced via WorkerMoodOverlay, or wire it into the sidebar if it was meant to be the canonical UI. Do NOT touch the PreferenceRequest type/store — those are actively used.

src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.title: Second 'Reset Simulation' button (active new-UI panel) also wipes localStorage + reloads without confirmation
src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.detail: Lines 458-473: red 'Reset Simulation' button's onClick removes millos-settings/millos-game-simulation/millos-has-played/millos-ai-narration/millos-knowledge then `setGraphicsQuality('medium'); window.location.reload();`. No confirmation. This is the panel surfaced by the current Dock/Sidebar UI, so it is the one most users will actually hit.
src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.fix: Add `if (!window.confirm('Reset the simulation and clear all saved data? This cannot be undone.')) return;` at the top of the onClick handler.

src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.title: 'Reset Simulation' removes a nonexistent key ('millos-settings') and leaves graphics + plaintext Gemini API key persisted
src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.detail: Line 461 removes 'millos-settings', but no zustand store uses that name (confirmed: actual keys are millos-graphics, millos-ai-config, etc.). The reset never removes 'millos-graphics' (real graphics key) nor 'millos-ai-config' (which holds the plaintext geminiApiKey via partialize at aiConfigStore.ts:558). So a 'reset' leaves the user's saved API key and graphics tweaks behind — a correctness and privacy gap (user reasonably expects reset to clear stored credentials).
src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.fix: Replace 'millos-settings' with 'millos-graphics' and add 'millos-ai-config' (plus other millos-* persist keys: millos-bas, millos-safety, millos-ui, etc.) to the removeItem list, or iterate `Object.keys(localStorage).filter(k => k.startsWith('millos-')).forEach(k => localStorage.removeItem(k))`.

src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.title: Active Settings panel has no entry point to AI / Gemini configuration
src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.detail: The new-UI SettingsPanel (the one mounted by the Dock) covers Simulation/Audio/Knowledge/Graphics/Reset but contains zero link to Gemini AI settings (grep for 'Gemini'/'API Key' in this file returns nothing). The only way to reach GeminiSettingsModal is a small button buried inside the AI Command Center sidebar panel (AICommandCenter.tsx:200-212). A user looking for 'where do I add my API key' in Settings finds nothing, and there is no first-run prompt — so the headline AI feature is effectively undiscoverable for new users.
src/components/ui-new/panels/SettingsPan.fix: Add an 'AI Assistant' section to SettingsPanel with a button that opens GeminiSettingsModal (or deep-links to the AI Command Center), mirroring the existing button in AICommandCenter.tsx:200.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: Modal lists wrong keyboard shortcuts (H/V/C) that do something else entirely
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: VisualizationToggles advertises `kbd` shortcuts that conflict with the real handlers in useKeyboardShortcuts.ts: 'Shift Handover' shows key 'H' (line 67) but H toggles the heatmap (useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:218) and shift-handover has NO key handler; 'VCL Context' shows key 'V' (line 62) but V toggles first-person mode (line 388); 'API Cost Tracker' shows key 'C' (line 59) but C toggles auto-rotation (line 331) — the cost overlay is actually on '$' (line 284). The modal's own caption (line 95) says 'Toggle overlays with keyboard shortcuts or buttons above', so users pressing H/V/C get surprising behavior.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Remove the incorrect `key` chips for Shift Handover, VCL Context, and API Cost Tracker (or wire real handlers). For API Cost Tracker change the chip to '$' to match useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:284. Verify each remaining chip against the actual handler keys.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: No disclosure that the Gemini API key is stored in plaintext localStorage
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: The API-key input (lines 254-264) and surrounding copy never warn that the entered key is persisted unencrypted in localStorage under 'millos-ai-config' (partialize at aiConfigStore.ts:555-560 persists geminiApiKey verbatim). On a shared/public machine this is a real privacy exposure; users entering a billable Google key deserve to know it is stored client-side in plaintext and persists across sessions. There is also no 'don't remember / session-only' option.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Add a short note under the input, e.g. 'Your key is stored in this browser only (localStorage), in plain text. Use "Clear Config" to remove it.' Optionally offer a session-only (non-persisted) toggle.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: Save button validates+saves silently; no persistence-success feedback for the no-key path and no plaintext-storage acknowledgement
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: handleSave (lines 148-166) shows a transient 'API key saved and connected!' toast then auto-closes after 1500ms (line 160). That is reasonable, but there's no indication the key was *persisted* (vs session-only), and the success toast disappears on auto-close before a user can register what happened. Combined with the missing plaintext disclosure, users get no durable confirmation of what was stored where.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Keep a persistent 'Connected • <masked key> (saved in this browser)' status (the masked-key line at modal lines 234-239 already exists for the connected state — ensure it remains visible and mentions local persistence) rather than relying on the auto-dismissing toast.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: GeminiSettingsModal exit animation never plays (early return before AnimatePresence)
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: Same antipattern as KeyboardShortcutsModal: line 174 `if (!isOpen) return null;` sits *above* the `<AnimatePresence>` at line 177. The backdrop and dialog declare `exit={{ opacity: 0 }}` (181) and `exit={{ scale: 0.9, opacity: 0 }}` (187), but because the whole component returns null when isOpen is false, AnimatePresence has no child to animate out. Opens with fade/scale; closes instantly with no exit transition. The modal is also self-mounted (rendered unconditionally by AICommandCenter.tsx:371), so the exit is genuinely lost.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Move the early return out: keep `<AnimatePresence>` always rendered and gate its child with `{isOpen && (<motion.div ...>...)}` so the exit animation can run, matching AboutModal.tsx.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: Centered modals lack focus trap / Escape-to-close that the reference AboutModal has
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: AboutModal.tsx:4,19 uses `useFocusTrap(modalRef, isOpen, onClose)` giving it a focus trap AND Escape-to-close. GeminiSettingsModal (closes only on backdrop click, line 183) and KeyboardShortcutsModal (closes only on backdrop click, line 78) have neither: keyboard focus can leave the dialog and Escape does not dismiss them. This is primarily a11y/interaction (may overlap another lane) but produces an inconsistent modal experience versus the established AboutModal pattern.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Apply the existing `useFocusTrap` hook to GeminiSettingsModal and KeyboardShortcutsModal (passing modalRef + isOpen + onClose), matching AboutModal. Adds Escape-close and focus containment with no visual change.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: GeminiSettingsModal lacks dialog semantics + focus trap (sibling AboutModal has them)
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: The modal container is a plain `<motion.div ... className="relative w-full max-w-md ...">` (line 185) with no `role="dialog"`, no `aria-modal="true"`, no `aria-labelledby`, and no focus trap / ESC handler / return-focus. There is no `useFocusTrap` call and no `onKeyDown` Escape handler anywhere in the file. The h2 'Gemini AI Settings' (line 196) is not wired as the accessible name. Keyboard users cannot ESC-close, focus is not trapped inside, and focus is not returned on close. The repo already has a working `useFocusTrap(ref, isOpen, onClose)` hook (src/hooks/useFocusTrap.ts) that AboutModal.tsx:19 uses for exactly this.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Mirror AboutModal: add `const modalRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement|null>(null); useFocusTrap(modalRef as React.RefObject<HTMLElement>, isOpen, onClose);`. On the inner motion.div (line 185) add `ref={modalRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="gemini-settings-title"` and give the h2 (line 196) `id="gemini-settings-title"`. This changes runtime behavior (ESC closes, focus traps), so it is high-value but not silent.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: GeminiSettingsModal close (X) button has no accessible name
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: Icon-only close button: `<button onClick={onClose} className="p-1 rounded-lg hover:bg-slate-800 transition-colors"><X className="w-5 h-5 text-slate-400" /></button>` (lines 198-203). No `aria-label` and no visible text, so screen readers announce only 'button'. AboutModal.tsx:54-60 sets `aria-label="Close about dialog"` and `aria-hidden="true"` on the X icon for the same control.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Add `aria-label="Close settings"` to the button (line 198) and `aria-hidden="true"` to the `<X />` icon (line 202). Pure SR fix, no visual/UX change.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: API key input label not programmatically associated with the input
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: `<label className="block text-sm font-medium text-slate-300">Google API Key</label>` (line 251) is a bare label with no `htmlFor`, and the password `<input type="password" value={inputKey} ...>` (line 254) has no `id` and no `aria-label`. The label is not associated with the field, so screen readers do not announce 'Google API Key' when the input is focused. (The 'AI Visualization Overlays' label at line 73 and 'AI Operating Mode' at line 306 are also non-associated, but those wrap button groups; line 251 is the real form-control case.)
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Give the input `id="gemini-api-key"` and the label `htmlFor="gemini-api-key"` (or add `aria-label="Google API Key"` to the input). Pure SR fix, no visual/UX change.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: Connection test result / error not announced to screen readers (no aria-live)
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail:
The test-result banner `{testResult && (...{testResult.message})}` (line 280-301) and the connection-error block `{connectionError && (
{connectionError}
)}` (line 241-246) appear/update visually but carry no `role="alert"` or `aria-live`. A keyboard/SR user clicking 'Test Connection' or 'Save & Connect' receives no announcement of success or failure. This is the form error-association checklist item made concrete.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Add `role="alert"` (or `aria-live="assertive"`) to the testResult motion.div (line 281) and `role="alert"` to the connectionError div (line 242). Pure SR fix, no visual/UX change.

src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: API-key field has no privacy note
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: Only the AI Studio link (266-276). Key stored plaintext in localStorage millos-ai-config (aiConfigStore.ts:553); no note it stays in-browser and MillOS never receives it.
src/components/GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Add note: stored only in this browser, sent direct to Google, MillOS never receives it.

src/App.tsx.title: Screen-reader keyboard notice advertises 'B for management' but B has no handler
src/App.tsx.detail: Line 581 (sr-only role=note): 'Use keyboard shortcuts: I for AI panel, O for SCADA, B for management, Escape to close panels.' There is no 'B' (plain) shortcut in useKeyboardShortcuts.ts — the B asset-browser handler is commented out (lines 351-359) and only Ctrl+B exists (Blueprint Mode, line 341). A screen-reader user pressing B to open 'management' gets nothing. This is the WCAG-facing help text, so accuracy matters most here.
src/App.tsx.fix: Remove 'B for management' from the notice (management is reached via the Dock, not a key), or wire a real 'B' handler. Keep the I/O/V/1-5 entries which are accurate.

src/App.tsx.title: No WebGL-unsupported detection; misleading error
src/App.tsx.detail: Absent-WebGL browser gets 'WebGL Context Lost' + Reload dead-end with wrong copy.
src/App.tsx.fix: getContext('webgl') check before Canvas; unsupported-browser screen.

src/hooks/useKnowledgeIntegration.ts.title: First-run welcome flag is set even when narration is disabled, silently burning the only first-play moment
src/hooks/useKnowledgeIntegration.ts.detail: The first-play effect (lines 88-109) writes `localStorage.setItem('millos-has-played', 'true')` (line 98) BEFORE/independently of whether the narration actually fires — triggerNarration (line 69-83) early-returns if `!FEATURE_FLAGS.AI_NARRATION_ENABLED || !narrationEnabled`. So if a user has AI Reflections turned off (toggleable in SettingsPanel:223-231) on first load, the welcome is permanently marked 'shown' and never appears even after they enable it. The flag should be set only when the welcome is genuinely delivered.
src/hooks/useKnowledgeIntegration.ts.fix: Move the `localStorage.setItem('millos-has-played','true')` and `hasTriggeredFirstPlay=true` writes inside the success path (after a narration is actually returned/shown), or gate the whole first-play effect on narration being enabled so the flag isn't consumed while disabled.

src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.title: Debug overlays default ON for end users (VCL Context + Shift Handover)
src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.detail: Lines 330 and 334: `showVCLDebug: true` and `showShiftHandover: true` default to enabled, while every other AI visualization toggle defaults false (lines 327-333 comment explicitly says 'all default OFF'). showVCLDebug in particular reads as a developer debug overlay; shipping it on by default clutters a first-time user's screen and contradicts the stated 'all default OFF' intent.
src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.fix: Set showVCLDebug to false (and reconsider showShiftHandover) so the launch experience is clean; let users opt in via the toggles. Confirm with owner whether Shift Handover is intended-on.

src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.title: Runtime Gemini failures (429/quota/network) never surface to the user — connectionError set only at setup time
src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.detail: connectionError is written only during key validation (aiConfigStore.ts:487, :497) and cleared on success (:509). There is NO distinct handling for 429 / RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED / quota anywhere (grep: zero hits for 429/quota/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED across src). Once connected, every runtime failure path — geminiClient.generateContent catch (geminiClient.ts:318) and generateStrategicDecision catch (aiEngine.ts:3951) — only calls logger.* and returns null; the circuit breaker may open after 3 failures (geminiClient.ts:201) with no UI signal. Net effect: when the user's free-tier key hits a rate/quota limit, the strategic AI silently stops producing decisions and the UI keeps showing 'connected' with no explanation. isConnected() at :158 also reports false once the breaker trips, but nothing maps that to a user-facing message.
src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.fix: On runtime failure in generateStrategicDecision (and/or in geminiClient.generateContent), categorize the error (rate-limit/quota vs auth vs network) and surface it: call a new aiConfigStore action to set connectionError (e.g. 'Gemini rate limit reached — AI paused, retrying in 30s') so GeminiSettingsModal/AICommandCenter can show it. Behavior change — flag for owner.

src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.title: Gemini API key persisted in plaintext localStorage with no user warning
src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.detail: partialize persists the raw key: `partialize: (state) => ({ aiMode: state.aiMode, geminiApiKey: state.geminiApiKey, managementGenerosity: state.managementGenerosity })` under store name 'millos-ai-config' (line 553). safeJSONStorage (storage.ts:43-58) writes to window.localStorage with no encryption/obfuscation. Any XSS, malicious browser extension, or shared-machine access reads the key verbatim. GeminiSettingsModal.tsx has no warning text about local plaintext storage (grep for 'plaintext|localStorage|not secure' returns nothing). This is a user-billing-cost exposure (the key is billable).
src/stores/aiConfigStore.ts.fix: Product decision needed: either (a) add a one-line caution under the API key input in GeminiSettingsModal.tsx ('Your key is stored unencrypted in this browser only and never sent to our servers'), or (b) move geminiApiKey to sessionStorage (cleared on tab close) by giving the persist config a separate storage, or (c) drop geminiApiKey from partialize so it lives only in memory for the session. Each changes UX/persistence behavior.

src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.title: FiveAxesPanel (z-40, top-20 right-4, w-80) can overlap the forklift info panel (z-50, top-20 right-4)
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.detail: UIOverlay.tsx:1993 places FiveAxesPanel at `fixed top-20 right-4 z-40 ... w-80`. App.tsx:610 renders the selected-forklift panel at `fixed right-4 top-20 ... z-50 min-w-64`. Both anchor to the exact same top-right corner; when a forklift is selected while the axes panel is open, they stack on the same pixels (forklift wins on z, but the axes panel is partially occluded with no offset). Not a crash, but a visible overlap on smaller viewports.
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.fix: Offset one panel vertically (e.g. give the forklift panel a different top, or collapse/hide FiveAxesPanel when a forklift is selected). Confirm visually since 3D-adjacent overlays aren't test-covered.

src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.title: Version-switcher <select> value 'v0.3' matches no <option>; renders blank + React warning
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.detail: The controlled select declares value="v0.3" but its options are value="v0.30", "v0.20", "v0.10": `<select ... value="v0.3"> ... <option value="v0.30">0.30</option>`. No option equals 'v0.3', so the browser resolves selectedIndex to -1 and shows a blank/indeterminate dropdown in the header, and React emits a dev console warning ('value provided ... no matching option'). The current version is visually missing from the version switcher.
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.fix: Change value to a real option that represents the running build, e.g. `value="v0.30"` (matching the first option), so the dropdown reflects the active version.

src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.title: MachineInfoPanel close button has no accessible name (announces as "multiplication sign")
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.detail:
The close button's only content is the "×" character and it has no aria-label or title:
```
<button
  onClick={onClose}
  className="text-slate-500 hover:text-white w-6 h-6 flex items-center justify-center rounded hover:bg-slate-800 transition-colors"
>
  ×
</button>
```
Per the accessible-name computation, element content wins, so screen readers announce this button as "× , button" (literally "multiplication sign"), giving no indication it closes the panel. The in-repo correct pattern is WorkerDetailPanel.tsx:440 (`aria-label={`Close ${worker.name} details`}`).
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.fix: Add `aria-label="Close machine details"` (and ideally `aria-hidden="true"` on the × glyph or replace with the Lucide `X` icon to match the no-emoji/icon convention).

src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.title: MachineInfoPanel status indicator conveys machine status by color alone (no text alternative)
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.detail:
The status dot encodes running/warning/critical purely via background color with no accompanying status text or aria-label:
```
<div
  className={`w-2 h-2 sm:w-2.5 sm:h-2.5 rounded-full ${machine.status === 'running' ? 'bg-green-500 animate-pulse' : machine.status === 'warning' ? 'bg-yellow-500' : 'bg-red-500'}`}
/>
```
A screen-reader (or colorblind) user has no way to perceive the machine status here. Contrast with WorkerDetailPanel.tsx:475-479, which pairs an aria-hidden color dot with visible status text. WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.fix: Add an accessible status label, e.g. `aria-label={`Status: ${machine.status}`}` on the dot div, or render the status text alongside the machine name (the panel currently shows name/type/id but not status as text).

src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.title: Version-switcher <select> has no programmatic label (name via title only)
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.detail: The version dropdown inside the <h1> (lines 1500-1511) has only a `title="Switch version"` and no `<label>` or `aria-label`. `title` is a valid name source but is unreliable on touch devices and some screen readers; it also navigates the whole page on change (`window.location.href`) with no warning. Nested interactive control inside an h1 is also unusual for heading semantics.
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.fix: Add `aria-label="Switch app version"` to the <select>. (Optional, owner call: move it out of the <h1> so the heading text isn't an interactive container.)

src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.title: MachineInfoPanel popup lacks dialog role / labelled region
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.detail: The machine info popup (motion.div at 2043) is a focused contextual panel with a heading (`<h2>` at 2062) and a close button, but it carries no `role="dialog"`/`aria-labelledby` and is not announced as a distinct region when opened. Unlike WorkerDetailPanel (role="dialog" + focus trap at 426/314), this panel is not clearly modal — adding role="dialog"/aria-modal would also imply a focus-trap obligation. Flagging for an owner decision rather than asserting a hard violation; the close-name and status-color findings above stand independently.
src/components/UIOverlay.tsx.fix: Owner decision: either (a) treat as non-modal and add `role="region" aria-labelledby` pointing at the h2 (give the h2 an id), or (b) make it a proper dialog with role="dialog"/aria-modal + focus trap mirroring WorkerDetailPanel. Do not add dialog role without the focus-trap.

src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts.title: Cost-overlay shortcut '$' (Shift+4) collides with the F4 'ultra' quality intent and is undiscoverable
src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts.detail: Line 284 binds the cost overlay to `key === '$'` (Shift+4). This shortcut appears in NO help surface (GraphicsSettingsPanel cheat-sheet, GeminiModal toggles list which wrongly says 'C', or App.tsx sr-only notice). A Shift+symbol shortcut is the least discoverable kind and is documented nowhere correctly, so the cost tracker is practically un-toggleable by keyboard.
src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts.fix: Either document '$' consistently across the GeminiModal toggle chip (currently 'C', line 59) and the cheat-sheet, or rebind to an unused letter and update all three help surfaces together.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Host never relays PLAYER_UPDATE → remote players frozen at spawn in 3+ player sessions
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: Topology is a star: guests connect only to the host (SignalingService.connectToHost, line 117). On receiving a guest's position the host only updates its own store and stops: `case 'PLAYER_UPDATE': store.updateRemotePlayer(message.payload.id, message.payload); break;` — no rebroadcast to other peers. The periodic STATE_SYNC payload (lines 448-458) carries `machines/gameTime/weather/emergencyActive` but NO player positions. So guest A's movement reaches the host and dies there; guest B never receives it and renders A frozen at the spawn position `[0, 1.7, 0]` set in addRemotePlayer (line 178) / PLAYER_JOIN handler (line 292). Two-player sessions work (host-guest is direct), which masks the bug.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: In the host branch of the PLAYER_UPDATE case, after updating the store, rebroadcast the update to all other peers excluding the sender: `if (store.isHost) this.broadcast(message, peerId);`. Verify against the message contract before shipping.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Join-timeout destroys singleton without nulling it → subsequent multiplayer attempts use a poisoned manager
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: joinRoom's connection-timeout callback calls `this.destroy()` (line 103) which sets `isDestroyed = true` but does NOT null the module-level `multiplayerManager` singleton — only the exported `destroyMultiplayerManager()` (line 680) does. After a join times out, the next `getMultiplayerManager()` (line 673) returns the same poisoned instance, and every broadcast/state-sync interval body short-circuits via `if (this.isDestroyed) return` (lines 419, 443, 464). Normal leave is fine because UI calls leave() then destroyMultiplayerManager() (MultiplayerLobby.tsx:99-100, MultiplayerPanel.tsx:130-131); the internal destroy() paths (joinRoom timeout, and any future internal teardown) are the gap.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: Have internal teardown null the singleton: call the module-level destroyMultiplayerManager() from the joinRoom timeout instead of this.destroy(), or add an isDestroyed reset/recreate guard in getMultiplayerManager() so a destroyed instance is replaced. Confirm no double-destroy regression first.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Stale-peer sweep drops a still-open DataConnection without closing it (leak on disconnect)
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: The ping interval detects stale peers and routes them to handlePeerDisconnected (lines 486-487). handlePeerDisconnected only does `this.peerConnections.delete(peerId)` (line 274) and never calls `peerConn.close()`. On the normal 'close' event path the underlying DataConnection is already closing, but on the STALE path the connection is still open (stale = no messages for 5s, not closed). The PeerConnection wrapper and its underlying WebRTC DataConnection are dropped from the map while still open, leaking the connection and its handlers.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: In handlePeerDisconnected (or before deletion in the stale sweep), close the wrapper: `this.peerConnections.get(peerId)?.close();` prior to `this.peerConnections.delete(peerId);`. PeerConnection.close() already guards isOpen and calls connection.close().

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Host applies untrusted peer MACHINE_LOCK/PLAYER_JOIN/PLAYER_LEAVE without authority validation → desync vector
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: handleMessage trusts message.payload for every type with no validation. A malicious/buggy guest can send `{type:'MACHINE_LOCK', payload:{machineId, playerId}}` and the host applies `store.setMachineLock(message.payload.machineId, message.payload.playerId)` (lines 350-351) directly, bypassing the intent-handler authority check (machine-lock is otherwise routed through ADJUST intents in handleMachineIntent, useMultiplayerSync.ts:88-94). Similarly a guest can inject arbitrary PLAYER_JOIN/PLAYER_LEAVE (lines 288-303) to add/remove phantom players in everyone's store. Peers are untrusted (PeerJS WebRTC). This is primarily a state-consistency/desync correctness issue (and an authority bug); not a server-auth concern since there is no backend.
