src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: On the host, gate MACHINE_LOCK/PLAYER_JOIN/PLAYER_LEAVE to host-authored broadcasts only: ignore inbound MACHINE_LOCK from guests (require it to flow via INTENT/handleMachineIntent), and ignore guest-originated PLAYER_JOIN/LEAVE. Validate payload.playerId matches the sender's connection identity. Behavior/contract change — owner decision.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: handlePeerDisconnected can run twice for one peer (onClose + stale sweep) → double PLAYER_LEAVE broadcast
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: A peer can be torn down via two independent paths: the PeerConnection onClose callback wired in handlePeerConnected (line 146 `onClose: () => this.handlePeerDisconnected(peerId)`) and the stale-connection sweep in the ping interval (lines 486-487). There is no guard that the peer is still present before acting, so for a peer that goes stale and then fires 'close', handlePeerDisconnected runs twice: it broadcasts PLAYER_LEAVE twice (lines 262-265) and calls store.removeRemotePlayer/removePeer twice. Store removals are idempotent, but the duplicate PLAYER_LEAVE broadcast is wasted/again-untrusted traffic.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: Early-return if the peer is already gone: `if (!this.peerConnections.has(peerId)) return;` at the top of handlePeerDisconnected.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Chat messages broadcast & stored unsanitized (sanitizeChatMessage never wired in)
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: sendChat() builds `const chatMessage = { ... message, ... }` from the raw string and both stores it locally (store.addChatMessage at line 608) and broadcasts it to peers (lines 611-614) with no sanitization. The matching receive path at line 354-355 `case 'CHAT': store.addChatMessage(message.payload);` stores raw peer-supplied content. sanitizeChatMessage() (sanitize.ts:178, strips \x00-\x1F\x7F control bytes, trims, slices to 500, HTML-encodes) exists and is exported but has ZERO callers. The UI maxLength=200 (MultiplayerChat.tsx:153) is a local-input-only attribute; untrusted remote peers can send arbitrarily long messages with control characters straight into the store. React text interpolation ({msg.message}) blunts direct-DOM XSS, so this is defense-in-depth + DoS/control-char hygiene rather than active script execution.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: In sendChat(), sanitize before building chatMessage: `const clean = sanitizeChatMessage(message); if (!clean) return; ... message: clean`. In handleMessage() CHAT case, sanitize on receive: `store.addChatMessage({ ...message.payload, message: sanitizeChatMessage(message.payload.message) });`. Import sanitizeChatMessage from '../utils'.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Player names from peer metadata stored unsanitized (sanitizePlayerName never wired in)
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: handlePeerConnected reads `const playerName = metadata?.playerName || 'Player';` (line 141) from untrusted PeerJS connection metadata and stores it via store.addRemotePlayer/addPeer (lines 175-193) and re-broadcasts it (lines 228-238) with no sanitization. The PLAYER_JOIN receive handler does the same: `name: message.payload.name` (line 291). sanitizePlayerName() (sanitize.ts:162, caps to 20 chars, alnum/_/-/space only) exists and is exported but has ZERO callers. A malicious peer can inject arbitrary-length names with control chars that propagate to all clients and render in the chat/lobby UI.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: Wrap untrusted names: at line 141 `const playerName = sanitizePlayerName(metadata?.playerName) || 'Player';` and at line 291 `name: sanitizePlayerName(message.payload.name) || 'Player'`. Import sanitizePlayerName from '../utils'.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Host trusts client-supplied playerId in INTENT (player impersonation / authority bypass)
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: case 'INTENT': const result = this.onMachineIntent(message.payload). The host executes message.payload (a MachineIntent) whose playerId field is fully attacker-controlled by the sending peer. Nothing binds the intent's playerId to the authenticated `peerId` of the connection it arrived on. A malicious guest can submit intents tagged with another player's id (or the host's id), defeating any per-player attribution, machine-lock ownership, or rate logic in the intent handler. Same trust gap exists in handlePeerDisconnected (line 252-255) which removes a player based on connection metadata.playerId, and in handleMessage PLAYER_LEAVE (line 302-303) where any peer can send PLAYER_LEAVE with an arbitrary id to evict another player from the host's store.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: On the host, override the trusted identity: build the effective intent from the connection's authenticated peerId rather than the payload, e.g. const trustedPlayerId = this.peerIdToPlayerId.get(peerId); this.onMachineIntent({...message.payload, playerId: trustedPlayerId}). For PLAYER_LEAVE, ignore the payload id when isHost and use the peerId mapping. Maintain a peerId->playerId map populated from the verified connection at join time.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: PLAYER_UPDATE/PLAYER_JOIN let any peer mutate arbitrary remote-player entries by id
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: case 'PLAYER_UPDATE': store.updateRemotePlayer(message.payload.id, message.payload). The id is taken from the untrusted payload, not from the authenticated peerId. A peer can send PLAYER_UPDATE with id set to another player's id and overwrite their position/name/color/selectedMachineId, or PLAYER_JOIN (line 288-300) to inject phantom players. There is no check that payload.id === the sending connection's player. In a host-authority model only the host should originate PLAYER_JOIN/UPDATE about other players; a guest should only be able to update its own entry.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: When isHost, derive the player id from the connection's peerId map and ignore payload.id for PLAYER_UPDATE from guests; only accept updates where payload.id maps to that peer. Treat PLAYER_JOIN/PLAYER_LEAVE as host-authoritative: guests must not be able to inject them (drop these message types when received from a non-host peer).

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: CHAT message stored from untrusted payload with attacker-controlled 'from'/'fromName' and no length bound
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: case 'CHAT': store.addChatMessage(message.payload). The entire ChatMessage (id, from, fromName, message, timestamp) is taken from the untrusted peer with no validation. from/fromName can be spoofed to impersonate another player or the host in the chat UI, and message has no length cap (DoS / store bloat). Rendering is via React JSX (MultiplayerChat.tsx auto-escapes, so no DOM XSS), but identity spoofing in chat and unbounded growth remain.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: Validate CHAT payload shape and cap message length (e.g. 500 chars). On the host, overwrite from/fromName with the sender's authenticated peer identity before re-broadcasting, so guests cannot forge sender identity.

src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.title: Guest accepts FULL_STATE_SYNC/STATE_SYNC from any connected peer, not verified-as-host
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.detail: case 'FULL_STATE_SYNC' / 'STATE_SYNC' only check `!store.isHost` before applying the payload to the production store (applyFullState/applyStateDiff dispatch window CustomEvents that drive machine state). They do not verify the message actually came from the host connection. In the current topology a guest connects only to the host, but PeerJS allows any peer to open a DataConnection to the guest's peer-id (`millos-${roomCode}-${playerId}`, predictable). Such a peer could push a forged FULL_STATE_SYNC to overwrite the guest's machine state, weather, and machine locks.
src/multiplayer/MultiplayerManager.ts.fix: Record the host's peerId on join and, in these branches, verify the originating peerId matches the known host before applying. Drop state-sync messages from any non-host peer.

src/scada/adapters/MQTTAdapter.ts.title: MQTT packets use single-byte remaining-length; payloads >127 bytes are malformed (encode + decode)
src/scada/adapters/MQTTAdapter.ts.detail: MQTT 'Remaining Length' is a variable-length quantity (1-4 bytes). The encoder writes it as one byte: `packet[1] = remainingLength;` in sendConnectPacket (line 234), publish (line 183), sendSubscribePacket (line 252), sendUnsubscribePacket (line 275). For any packet whose remaining length exceeds 127 this silently truncates/corrupts the length field. The receiver mirrors the bug: handlePublish reads `const remainingLength = bytes[offset++];` (line 303, comment admits 'simplified - assumes single byte') and then `payload = ...slice(offset, 2 + remainingLength)` (line 314), so multi-byte length tag payloads are mis-parsed. Real protocol bug; low real-world severity because the shipped static deploy is simulation-first and a live MQTT broker is unlikely to be reachable (no broker/CORS in the GitHub-Pages config).
src/scada/adapters/MQTTAdapter.ts.fix: Implement proper MQTT variable-length-integer encoding/decoding (continuation-bit scheme) for both the writer (packet[1]...) and handlePublish's remaining-length read. Behavior-changing protocol work — owner decision.

src/scada/SCADABridge.ts.title: calculateMachineVisuals omits machineName, so name-only machines resolve no SCADA prefix (visuals blank while store metrics sync)
src/scada/SCADABridge.ts.detail: scadaToStoreMetrics passes machineName to getMachineTagPrefix (line 390, called as `scadaToStoreMetrics(machine.id, ..., machine.name)` in store.ts:179), enabling the display-name fallbacks for 'RM-101'/'SIFTER A'/'PACK LINE N' (getMachineTagPrefix lines 168-197). But calculateMachineVisuals calls `getMachineTagPrefix(machineId)` with no name (line 219, invoked from useSCADAVisuals.ts:30/47). For any machine whose id does NOT match the id-prefix patterns (silo-/rm-/sifter-/packer-) but whose name would, the prefix is null in calculateMachineVisuals → no temperature/vibration/rpm visuals, while scadaToStoreMetrics still syncs metrics. Inconsistent behavior between the two paths. Only bites if such name-only machines exist (roster ids not in types.ts/store.ts inline, unverified).
src/scada/SCADABridge.ts.fix: Thread machineName through calculateMachineVisuals and pass it to getMachineTagPrefix, matching scadaToStoreMetrics. Confirm a name-only machine actually exists before treating as more than a consistency cleanup.

src/scada/SCADABridge.ts.title: applyCorrelations divides by engHigh-engLow with no guard — NaN/Infinity if a correlated tag has zero range
src/scada/SCADABridge.ts.detail: `const normalizedCorrelated = ((correlatedValue.value as number) - correlatedDef.engLow) / (correlatedDef.engHigh - correlatedDef.engLow);` (lines 497-499). If a correlated tag definition has engHigh === engLow (degenerate/misconfigured range), this is division by zero → Infinity/NaN, which then propagates into the simulated value via correlationEffect (line 502-505) and ultimately into TagValue.value. Although tick() later clamps to engLow/engHigh of the *target* tag (SimulationAdapter line 411), a NaN survives Math.max/Math.min as NaN and can reach the 3D bridge.
src/scada/SCADABridge.ts.fix: Guard the denominator: `const range = correlatedDef.engHigh - correlatedDef.engLow; if (range <= 0) return;` (skip that correlation) before computing normalizedCorrelated.

src/multiplayer/hooks/useMultiplayerSync.title: applyStateDiff treats the host's full STATE_SYNC as a diff; sequence number is ignored and emergencyActive is never applied on guests
src/multiplayer/hooks/useMultiplayerSync.detail: The host's STATE_SYNC payload (MultiplayerManager.ts:448-458) is a full snapshot (entire machines array + sequence + timestamp), not a diff, but is consumed by applyStateDiff as GameStateDiff. The `sequence` field is dropped (no ordering/staleness check — benign because the channel is reliable:true). More concretely, the emergencyActive branch is a no-op: `if (diff.emergencyActive && !gameStore.emergencyActive) { /* In a full implementation, this would trigger the full emergency sequence */ }` (lines 145-150) — the comment confirms guests never react to host emergency state, so a fire-drill/emergency started by the host does not propagate to guests.
src/multiplayer/hooks/useMultiplayerSync.fix: Implement the emergencyActive propagation on guests (call the gameSimulation emergency trigger) and rename/treat STATE_SYNC honestly as a snapshot. Behavior change — owner decision on the emergency-sync semantics.

src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.title: Lobby has no host-disconnect feedback
src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.detail: Lobby lacks host-disconnected listener Panel(57-82) has; guest silently dumped to join form.

src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.title: Player-name entry has no data-sharing disclosure before broadcasting the name to peers and broker
src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.detail: The name field collects free-text (up to 20 chars) and on Create/Join it is immediately stored and broadcast: `<input ... value={playerName} onChange={(e) => setPlayerName(e.target.value)} placeholder="Enter your name" maxLength={20} ... />` with no adjacent notice. handleCreateRoom/handleJoinRoom call setLocalPlayerName(playerName.trim()) and connect. Users may enter their real name without being told it is shared with untrusted peers (and sent as broker metadata). This is a transparency/data-minimization gap; the field also accepts arbitrary PII a user might volunteer.
src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.fix: Owner decision (UX copy): add a short helper line under the name input noting the name is visible to other players in the room. Optionally suggest a nickname. No runtime-default behavior change required for the copy itself.

src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.title: Local player name & room code entered without sanitizePlayerName/sanitizeRoomCode
src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.detail: Name entry uses only `.trim()`: `setLocalPlayerName(playerName.trim())` and `manager.hostRoom(playerName.trim())` (lines 63-65), `manager.joinRoom(joinCode.trim().toUpperCase(), playerName.trim())` (line 89). Same pattern in MultiplayerPanel.tsx:94-96,118-120. sanitizePlayerName() (max 20, charset-restricted) and sanitizeRoomCode() (sanitize.ts:203, exactly 6 uppercase alnum) exist and are exported but unused at these entry points. Names of arbitrary length/charset enter the store and get broadcast to peers.
src/components/multiplayer/MultiplayerLo.fix: Replace `.trim()` with `sanitizePlayerName(playerName)` at the setLocalPlayerName/host/join call sites in both MultiplayerLobby.tsx and MultiplayerPanel.tsx, and gate join with `const code = sanitizeRoomCode(joinCode); if (!code) return;`. Import from '@/utils'. Two-file change but each edit is intra-file.

src/multiplayer/types.ts.title: reconnecting styled but never set
src/multiplayer/types.ts.detail: reconnecting styled in both UIs but never set; transient drop shows nothing.
src/multiplayer/types.ts.fix: Set in reconnect path or remove dead branches.

src/multiplayer/types.ts.title: ConnectionState 'reconnecting' is defined but never set — dead UI state / misleading reconnect contract
src/multiplayer/types.ts.detail: types.ts:20 declares `type ConnectionState = 'disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'reconnecting'`. SignalingService.ts:93-97 logs 'attempting reconnect' and relies on PeerJS auto-reconnect, but nothing in MultiplayerManager or the multiplayer store ever calls setConnectionState('reconnecting') (grep: no setter usage of the literal). On PeerJS 'disconnected', the guest path goes straight to handleHostDisconnect (MultiplayerManager.ts:271) / setConnectionState('disconnected') (:103) with no reconnecting phase. Any UI keyed on 'reconnecting' is dead code, and the auto-reconnect is invisible to the user.
src/multiplayer/types.ts.fix: Either wire setConnectionState('reconnecting') into the SignalingService 'disconnected' handler (and back to 'connected' on reopen) so the UI reflects the auto-reconnect, or remove the unused literal from the union. First option changes UX — flag for owner if chosen.

src/main.tsx.title: prototype eagerly bundled in shared entry
src/main.tsx.detail: Static import of AssetPrototypePage stops Rollup splitting 162KB; loads for every visitor.

src/main.tsx.title: No global unhandledrejection / window.onerror handler — async rejections outside React are invisible
src/main.tsx.detail: Grep across src for 'unhandledrejection' / 'window.onerror' / 'onunhandledrejection' returns zero matches. React's ErrorBoundary (main.tsx:53, App.tsx:651) only catches render-phase errors; it does NOT catch unhandled promise rejections (e.g. the Rapier prewarm at main.tsx:10, dynamic imports, audio resume, fetch in modelLoader) or async errors thrown outside the React tree. At launch these fail completely silently with no logging or telemetry, making field diagnosis impossible. main.tsx already monkeypatches console.warn (:17), so it is the natural place to add a listener.
src/main.tsx.fix: In main.tsx add window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection', e => logger.error('[unhandledrejection]', e.reason)) and a window.addEventListener('error', ...) so background async failures are at least logged. No UX change.

src/main.tsx.title: Rapier ~2.2MB WASM eagerly pre-warmed at module top-level on every page load
src/main.tsx.detail: Lines 10-14: `import('@dimforge/rapier3d-compat').then((RAPIER) => { RAPIER.init()... })` runs unconditionally at startup, downloading and instantiating the ~2.2MB Rapier WASM physics engine (the 'three-rapier' chunk, vite.config.ts:106) before any user action, competing for bandwidth with the React/three boot. The comment claims reduced perceived startup, but it front-loads 2.2MB onto the critical boot window even for users who never trigger physics.
src/main.tsx.fix: Gate the pre-warm behind requestIdleCallback or first-interaction so the visible app boot finishes before fetching/instantiating physics WASM. Behavior-sensitive (slight delay before first Physics mount), flag for owner.

src/prototypes/AssetPrototypePage.tsx.title: Internal deck public at /prototypes
src/prototypes/AssetPrototypePage.tsx.detail: 4200-line dev deck routable in prod. Posture question.

src/utils/pathfinding.ts.title: Dead A-star module, zero importers

src/utils/pathfinding.ts.fix: Delete or convert TODO to issue.

SettingsPanel.tsx.title: Reset clears 5 of 16 keys; plaintext Gemini key survives
SettingsPanel.tsx.detail: Reset removes 5 millos keys, leaves millos-ai-config plaintext key (aiConfigStore.ts:553-560) and 10 others. GraphicsSettingsPanel.tsx:623-624 same. GDPR erasure gap.
SettingsPanel.tsx.fix: Add Clear all data removing every millos key or a Forget API key button.

GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.title: No disclosure plant data sent to Google Gemini
GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.detail: Only AI Studio link shown; factory state (aiEngine.ts:3437 via geminiClient.generateContent) sent to generativelanguage.googleapis.com undisclosed. GDPR transparency gap.
GeminiSettingsModal.tsx.fix: Add notice MillOS sends simulation state to Google Gemini under Google Privacy Policy.

src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.title: Internal `if (!isOpen) return null` defeats the AnimatePresence exit animation
src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.detail: The component defines exit transitions (`exit={{ opacity: 0 }}` line 76, `exit={{ scale: 0.9, opacity: 0 }}` line 86) and is mounted at the call site inside <AnimatePresence> (UIOverlay.tsx:1389-1393: `<AnimatePresence>{showShortcuts && <KeyboardShortcutsModal .../>}`). But line 17 `if (!isOpen) return null;` makes the component unmount itself synchronously the instant isOpen flips false, before AnimatePresence can play the exit. Result: open animates in, close pops out with no fade/scale-out. AboutModal.tsx:22-23 demonstrates the correct pattern (AnimatePresence wraps `{isOpen && ...}` *inside* the component).
src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.fix: Remove the internal `if (!isOpen) return null;` and instead wrap the returned JSX in `<AnimatePresence>{isOpen && (...)}</AnimatePresence>` inside the component (mirroring AboutModal), and drop the redundant outer AnimatePresence/`showShortcuts &&` gating in UIOverlay.tsx:1389-1393. Restructure, so verify the close path still mounts/unmounts.

src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.title: Modal lacks dialog role, aria-modal, focus trap, and ESC-to-close
src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.detail: The modal root `<motion.div ... className="fixed inset-0 z-[1000] ... " onClick={onClose}>` (line 72-79) and its content panel (line 83) have no role="dialog", no aria-modal="true", no aria-labelledby pointing at the title h2 (line 102), no focus trap, and no Escape key handler — even though the footer (lines 166-176) and its own shortcut list (line 56 'ESC: Close panels') tell users ESC closes panels. Keyboard/AT users cannot escape or are not told they are in a dialog. Violates WCAG 4.1.2, 2.1.2 (no keyboard trap requires proper handling), 2.4.3 focus order.
src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.fix: On the inner panel add role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="kbd-shortcuts-title" (give the h2 that id); add a useEffect keydown listener calling onClose() on 'Escape'; move initial focus into the dialog and restore on close (or trap focus). UX adds ESC behavior, so owner should confirm.

src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.title: Icon-only modal close button (X) has no accessible name
src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.detail: `<button onClick={onClose} className="w-8 h-8 rounded-lg ..."><X className="w-4 h-4" /></button>` (lines 108-117). The only child is the lucide X icon (decorative, no aria-hidden, no label), so the button has no accessible name. Compare EmergencyStopButton.tsx (aria-label + aria-hidden on icon) which the codebase already does correctly. Violates WCAG 4.1.2.
src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsModal.fix: Add aria-label="Close keyboard shortcuts" to the button and aria-hidden="true" to the X icon.

src/components/ui-new/hud/StatusHUD.tsx.title: Safety alerts render the generic blue 'info' dot, indistinguishable from info notifications
src/components/ui-new/hud/StatusHUD.tsx.detail: The notification-list status dot color chain handles only critical/warning/success then falls through to blue: `alert.type === 'critical' ? 'bg-red-500' : alert.type === 'warning' ? 'bg-amber-500' : alert.type === 'success' ? 'bg-green-500' : 'bg-blue-500'`. But AlertData.type includes 'safety' (src/types.ts:215) and AlertSystem.tsx:52-59 actively emits `type: 'safety'` near-miss alerts (the highest-attention category, paired with audioManager.playAlert()). These show a blue dot identical to ordinary 'info', so the most important alerts get no distinct visual treatment in the dropdown.
src/components/ui-new/hud/StatusHUD.tsx.fix: Add an explicit branch for safety, e.g. `: alert.type === 'safety' ? 'bg-orange-500'` (or a dedicated shield-tinted color) before the blue default, so safety/near-miss notifications are visually distinct.

src/components/AboutModal.tsx.title: Centered-modal backdrop opacity is inconsistent across modals (50/60/70)
src/components/AboutModal.tsx.detail: Three centered modals use three different backdrop darkness values for the same affordance: AboutModal.tsx:30 `bg-black/50`, KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx:81 `bg-black/60` (dark) / `bg-black/40` (light), GeminiSettingsModal.tsx:182 `bg-black/70`. Opening different dialogs dims the scene by visibly different amounts, an inconsistency in the modal scrim. All three correctly share `z-[1000]` and backdrop-blur, so only the opacity diverges.
src/components/AboutModal.tsx.fix: Pick one canonical scrim (e.g. `bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm`) and apply it to all centered modals; ideally extract a shared <ModalBackdrop> or constant. Low-risk visual standardization.

src/components/SCADAPanel.tsx.title: SCADA trend Tooltip sets dark contentStyle but no label text color (low-contrast label row)
src/components/SCADAPanel.tsx.detail: The recharts <Tooltip contentStyle={{ backgroundColor: '#1e293b', border:'1px solid #334155', borderRadius:'8px', fontSize:'12px' }}> sets a dark surface but no text/label color. Recharts colors the per-series *item* rows with the line stroke (cyan/green/etc, visible), but the *label* row (the formatted timestamp) uses recharts' default dark label color, which is low-contrast on #1e293b. Cannot confirm exact rendered contrast without running the app; flagging as a readability risk on the tooltip label only, not the whole tooltip.
src/components/SCADAPanel.tsx.fix: Add `labelStyle={{ color: '#e2e8f0' }}` (and optionally `itemStyle={{ color: '#cbd5e1' }}`) to the Tooltip so the timestamp label is legible on the dark background. Verify visually after change.

src/components/SCADAPanel.tsx.title: Trend line palette pairs deuteranopia-confusable green/red series
src/components/SCADAPanel.tsx.detail: `const TREND_COLORS = ['#06b6d4', '#10b981', '#f59e0b', '#ef4444', '#8b5cf6', '#ec4899'];` includes green (#10b981, idx 1) and red (#ef4444, idx 3), a classic red-green confusion pair for deuteranopia/protanopia. Mitigating factors: they are separated by amber (idx 2), and each line carries a text legend entry (Legend formatter at SCADAPanel.tsx:1021), so identification does not rely on color alone. Minor.
src/components/SCADAPanel.tsx.fix: Optional: swap to a color-blind-safe categorical palette (e.g. Okabe-Ito) for the 6 trend series. Low priority given legend labels already disambiguate.

src/components/WorkerDetailPanel.tsx.title: Non-embedded WorkerDetailPanel claims aria-modal but is a bare floating panel with a literal × close glyph (dead path)
src/components/WorkerDetailPanel.tsx.detail: In non-embedded mode the panel sets `role="dialog" aria-modal={!embedded}` (true) yet renders as `absolute bottom-6 left-6 w-96` with NO backdrop overlay and no focus trap, so aria-modal=true misrepresents a non-blocking floating panel. The close button uses a literal `×` character (line 443) instead of a Lucide `X` icon (CLAUDE.md mandates Lucide). NOTE: every shipping call site passes `embedded={true}` (ContextSidebar.tsx via the minified `n` alias), so this non-embedded branch is effectively dead — severity kept low.
src/components/WorkerDetailPanel.tsx.fix: Since the path is unused, either delete the non-embedded branch (dead-code) or, if kept, drop aria-modal for the floating variant and replace `×` with `<X className="w-4 h-4" />`. Confirm with owner whether non-embedded mode is intended to ship.

src/components/WorkerDetailPanel.tsx.title: Worker detail tabpanels reference tab IDs that do not exist (dangling aria-labelledby)
src/components/WorkerDetailPanel.tsx.detail: Each tabpanel back-references its tab via `aria-labelledby` (overview-panel: `aria-labelledby="overview-tab"` at line 523; skills 600; reviews 646; alignment 671), but the tab buttons rendered in the map at lines 496-511 carry `role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, and `aria-controls={`${tab.id}-panel`}` and have NO `id` attribute. So `aria-labelledby="overview-tab"` points at a nonexistent element — the panel→tab back-reference dangles for all 4 tabs. (The tab→panel direction via aria-controls is correct because the panels do have matching ids.)
src/components/WorkerDetailPanel.tsx.fix: Add `id={`${tab.id}-tab`}` to the tab button in the map at line 496-511 so the existing `aria-labelledby` references resolve.

src/components/ProductionMetrics.tsx.title: Throughput AreaChart has no empty/initial-data state (renders blank area on first paint)
src/components/ProductionMetrics.tsx.detail: The AreaChart at lines 279-293 binds `data={data}` where `data` is `useState<MetricsData[]>([])` (line 118) and is only populated by a mount effect (lines 200-214). On first render the chart area is empty with no placeholder, so the 'Production (30m)' card briefly shows a blank chart body under the 'Live' label. Unlike SCADAPanel which has an explicit empty state (SCADAPanel.tsx:984-989), this mini-chart shows nothing. Brief but visible flicker on panel open.
src/components/ProductionMetrics.tsx.fix: Render a subtle placeholder (e.g. a faint baseline or 'Collecting data…') when `data.length === 0`, or seed the first data point synchronously. Low risk; verify it doesn't disturb the sr-only data table.

src/components/ui/DecisionReplay.tsx.title: Decision-replay modal lacks dialog role/aria-modal/ESC/focus management
src/components/ui/DecisionReplay.tsx.detail: Modal root `<motion.div ... className="fixed inset-0 bg-black/60 ... z-[1000] flex items-center justify-center p-4" onClick={onClose}>` (line 71-77) and panel (line 78) have no role="dialog", aria-modal, aria-label, focus trap, or Escape-key handler. The close X (lines 96-101) is icon-only with no aria-label (`<X className="w-5 h-5 text-slate-400" />`). Violates WCAG 4.1.2, 2.1.2, 2.4.3.
src/components/ui/DecisionReplay.tsx.fix: Add role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Decision replay" to the panel; add Escape keydown -> onClose; add aria-label="Close" to the X button + aria-hidden on the icon; manage initial/return focus. ESC behavior is a UX addition.

src/components/ui/DecisionReplay.tsx.title: Clickable div trigger is not keyboard operable
src/components/ui/DecisionReplay.tsx.detail:
DecisionReplayTrigger wraps content in a clickable div: `
setShowReplay(true)} className="cursor-pointer">{children}
` (line 201). No role="button", tabIndex, or onKeyDown — keyboard users cannot open the replay. Violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and 4.1.2.

src/components/ui/TimelinePlayback.tsx.title: Replay controls: icon-only buttons (close, play/pause) lack accessible names
src/components/ui/TimelinePlayback.tsx.detail: The exit-replay button `<button onClick={exitReplay} ...><X className="w-4 h-4 ..." /></button>` (lines 124-130) and the play/pause toggle `<button onClick={() => setIsPlaying(!isPlaying)} ...>{isPlaying ? <PauseCircle.../> : <PlayCircle.../>}</button>` (lines 178-187) are icon-only with no aria-label. The SkipBack button has title="Jump to start" (line 174) but the close and play/pause have nothing. The range slider (lines 147-161) also has no aria-label. Violates WCAG 4.1.2 / 1.1.1.

src/components/ui/StrategicPriorityCards.title: Three icon-only dismiss buttons unlabeled; AI 'analyzing' status not announced
src/components/ui/StrategicPriorityCards.detail: Dismiss buttons at lines 100-105 (``), 140-145, and 166-171 are icon-only with no aria-label. Additionally the 'Gemini analyzing...' thinking indicator (lines 41-52, `Gemini analyzing...`) is dynamic status content with no aria-live region. Violates WCAG 4.1.2 and 4.1.3.
src/components/ui/StrategicPriorityCards.fix: Add aria-label="Dismiss priority" / "Dismiss insight" / "Dismiss trade-off" to the three X buttons; wrap the thinking indicator in a container with role="status" aria-live="polite".

src/components/ui/DecisionHistoryPanel.t.title: Pagination prev/next buttons are icon-only with no accessible name

Page indicator (line 133) is separate text. Violates WCAG 4.1.2.

src/components/ui/ZoneCustomizationPanel.title: Remove-zone button only visible on hover (not focus-visible) and unlabeled
src/components/ui/ZoneCustomizationPanel.detail: `<button onClick={() => removeSpeedZone(zone.id)} className="opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 p-1 transition-all ..." title="Remove zone">` (lines 72-79). It is `opacity-0` until mouse hover with no `focus:opacity-100` / `focus-within` rule, so a keyboard user who tabs to it gets a focused-but-invisible control (fails WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible). The icon child relies on title only, no aria-label (4.1.2).

The summary appears automatically on shift change but its container (header at ~line 200) has no role="status"/aria-live, so AT users are not notified it appeared. Violates WCAG 4.1.2 and 4.1.3.

src/components/ui/ConfidenceBar.tsx.title: Confidence progress bar has no progressbar role/value and uses color alone
src/components/ui/ConfidenceBar.tsx.detail:
The bar `
` (lines 57-64) has no role="progressbar", aria-valuenow/min/max, or aria-label, so AT cannot read the confidence value when showPercentage is false. Confidence level (high/med/low) is signaled only by bar color green/amber/red at the 80/60 thresholds (getColor, lines 29-33) with no non-color indicator — WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. This component is reused (e.g. StrategicPriorityCards); fix stays inside ConfidenceBar so blast radius is local.
